chapter: Chapter: Keeping Time with the Skies

questions.question: Why do shadows change their direction and length during the day?

options.A: Because the Moon moves around Earth
options.B: Because Earth rotates on its axis

questions.explanation: Earth’s rotation causes the Sun’s apparent position to change, altering shadow direction and length.

questions.question: Which phenomenon helped ancient people divide a month?
questions.explanation: Ancient civilizations used the changing phases of the Moon to measure months.

questions.question: Why does the Moon appear to change shape during the month?

options.B: Because different portions of the Moon are illuminated by the Sun
options.C: Because the Moon produces its own light

questions.explanation: The Moon’s phases occur because different parts of its sunlit surface become visible from Earth.

questions.question: What causes the apparent movement of stars across the night sky?
questions.explanation: Stars appear to move because Earth rotates on its axis.

questions.question: Which constellation is easily recognized by three stars forming a straight line?
questions.explanation: Orion has three bright stars forming Orion’s Belt.

questions.question: Why can different constellations be seen in different seasons?
questions.explanation: Earth’s revolution around the Sun changes the part of the sky visible at night.

questions.question: Which astronomical observation helped people develop early calendars?
questions.explanation: Lunar phases helped early civilizations track months and create calendars.

questions.question: What would happen if Earth stopped rotating?

options.A: There would be no day and night cycle

questions.explanation: Day and night occur due to Earth’s rotation.

questions.question: Which tool was used historically to measure time based on the Sun’s shadow?
questions.explanation: Sundials measure time using the shadow cast by the Sun.

questions.question: Which celestial body reflects sunlight and does not produce its own light?
questions.explanation: The Moon reflects sunlight and appears bright in the night sky.

questions.question: Why is the North Star useful for navigation?

options.A: It moves quickly across the sky
options.B: It always points toward the north direction

questions.explanation: Polaris is nearly aligned with Earth’s rotational axis and indicates north.

questions.question: What causes the Moon to rise and set each day?
questions.explanation: Earth’s rotation causes the Moon to appear to rise and set.

questions.question: Why do astronomers observe stars and planets regularly?

options.B: To understand celestial movements and time cycles

questions.explanation: Astronomical observations help understand celestial cycles and time measurement.

questions.question: Which motion of Earth causes seasons?
questions.explanation: Earth’s revolution around the Sun leads to seasonal changes.

questions.question: Why does the same constellation appear to move across the sky during the night?
questions.explanation: Earth’s rotation causes stars to appear to move across the sky.

questions.question: What helped ancient sailors determine direction at sea?
questions.explanation: The North Star helped sailors locate north and navigate.

questions.question: Which event marks the start of a lunar month?
questions.explanation: A lunar month begins with the New Moon phase.

questions.question: Why are stars not visible during daytime?
questions.explanation: The bright sunlight scatters in Earth’s atmosphere, hiding stars.

questions.question: What helps astronomers determine Earth’s position in space?
questions.explanation: Astronomers study stars and planets to understand Earth’s position and movement.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of studying constellations?
questions.explanation: Constellations help in navigation and identifying star patterns.

questions.question: Which celestial event occurs when the Moon is exactly between Earth and the Sun?
questions.explanation: A solar eclipse occurs when the Moon blocks sunlight reaching Earth.

questions.question: What would happen if Earth did not revolve around the Sun?
questions.explanation: Seasons occur due to Earth’s revolution around the Sun.

questions.question: Why do some stars appear brighter than others?

options.A: They are closer to Earth or larger

questions.explanation: Brightness depends on star size and distance from Earth.

questions.question: Which celestial body mainly determines a solar calendar?
questions.explanation: Solar calendars are based on Earth’s revolution around the Sun.

questions.question: Which observation helps scientists determine the length of a year?
questions.explanation: A year is defined by Earth’s revolution around the Sun.
